sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The green nodes are:
1. At rows 37-40, cols 18-21 (collected block)
2. At rows 57-60, cols 33-36 (bottom bar)
So the green target did NOT reappear on the right side. The green block is still collected at rows 37-40, cols 18-21.
Wait, I notice the green block on the bottom bar is at cols 33-36, not cols 39-42 like the blue block. Let me check the bottom bar positions:
- Pink (player): cols 27-30
- Red: cols 30-33
- Green: cols 33-36
- Blue: cols 39-42
Wait, that doesn't add up. Let me re-examine. The bottom bar is at rows 57-60. The blocks are:
- Pink: cols 27-30 (approximately)
- Red: cols 30-33
- Green: cols 33-36
- Blue: cols 39-42
Hmm, the positions don't match exactly. Let me check the segmentation more carefully.
Actually, I think I'm overcomplicating this. Let me reconsider the game mechanics.
When I extended the path from the player's position (rows 37-40), the green target appeared at the end of the path. But when I retracted the path, the green block remained at rows 37-40, cols 18-21. So the green target wasn't "uncollected".
Wait, but when I extended the path, the green block at the end of the path was at rows 37-40, cols 42-45 (approximately). This is the same position as the original green target. So when I extend the path from the player's position (rows 37-40), the green target reappears at its original position.
So the mechanic is:
1. When the player is at the same row position as a collected block, extending the path causes that target to reappear at its original position.
2. This allows me to "re-collect" the target, which might change its position in the collected stack.
But when I retracted the path, the green block remained at rows 37-40, cols 18-21. So the green target wasn't "uncollected" or "re-collected".
Hmm, maybe I need to extend the path to the target and then NOT retract it. Let me try extending the path again and see if the green target appears at the end of the path. Then I'll check if the collected block changes.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the previous state. When I extended the path from rows 37-40, the green target appeared at the end of the path (rows 37-40, cols 42-45). But the collected green block was still at rows 37-40, cols 18-21. So there were two green blocks: one collected and one at the end of the path.
When I retracted the path, the green block at the end of the path disappeared, and the collected green block remained at rows 37-40, cols 18-21.
So the mechanic is:
1. When the player is at the same row position as a collected block, extending the path causes that target to appear at the end of the path.
2. When the path is retracted, the target at the end of the path disappears, and the collected block remains.
This doesn't help me rearrange the collected blocks. Let me think about this differently.
Maybe the goal is not to rearrange the collected blocks, but to have the player at a specific position when all targets are collected. The bottom bar shows the order: red, green, blue. Maybe the player needs to be at the position corresponding to the last target (blue) when all targets are collected.
Currently, the player is at rows 37-40 (green position). The blue target was collected when the player was at rows 31-34. So maybe I need to move the player to rows 31-34 (blue position) to complete the level.
Let me try moving the player to rows 31-34 and see if the level completes.
